subject: Project Management
title: Hardware Development
description: A quick win: understand Hardware Development.

hook.text: Designing a microchip costs billions. You can't iterate cheaply. Waterfall is the only option.

buildup.text: Chip fabrication requires months of design verification before a single transistor is etched in silicon.

discovery.text: One error in chip design can cost $100 million to fix — the price of a new fabrication mask set.

twist.text: Hardware teams simulate obsessively: running millions of virtual tests before committing to physical production.

climax.text: When the cost of change is astronomically high, exhaustive upfront planning isn't bureaucracy — it's survival.

punchline.text: When one mistake costs $100 million, you plan very carefully.

quiz.question: Why is waterfall suited to hardware development?
options: Physical manufacturing errors are extremely expensive to fix
Hardware is easy to change after production
Agile doesn't work with any physical product
